volume.quantity_model_note: Includes the 2026 'Quantity Model' revisions for Hydrogen gas-measuring and EVSE (Electric Vehicle Supply Equipment) energy-to-volume equivalents.
volume.legal_metrology_warning: In 2026, commercial trade of liquid hydrogen requires a tolerance threshold of ±1.5% for delivery accuracy at designated stations.

industrial_automation.quantity_model_note: Implements the new 'Quantity Model' which formalizes EngineeringUnit Properties directly within the metadata layer for real-time edge processing.
industrial_automation.legal_metrology_warning: Field-level automation in 2026 must ensure that unit metadata persists across heterogenous network architectures to prevent data-type poisoning.

force.regulatory_benchmark: ISO/ASTM 52959:2026 AM - Lattice structure axial force validation
force.quantity_model_note: Specifies 2026 Quantity Model values for non-linear stress-strain tensors in metallic lattice designs for aerospace components.
force.legal_metrology_warning: Additive Manufacturing components for load-bearing applications must undergo May 2026 certified validation for axial force uniformity.

power.quantity_model_note: Standardizes the reporting of Reactive Power (VAr) vs Real Power (Watts) using the 2026 Quantity Model for phasor measurement units.
power.legal_metrology_warning: Smart Grid nodes active as of May 2026 are legally required to support bidirectional unit-conversion protocols for local secondary voltage control.

weight.quantity_model_note: Addresses the 2026 'Quantity Model' shift to automated mass verification in logistics centers using high-speed dynamic scale protocols.
weight.legal_metrology_warning: Commercial weighing devices calibrated in 2026 must demonstrate traceability to the revised SI kilogram definition within ±0.001% tolerance.
